text:
Centre for Neurology Services

Neurosciences Division

Clinic Date: 09 August 2018

Dr Sarah Nguyen
Consultant Neurologist
Riverside Neurology Clinic
12 Avon Way, Bristol BS1 4QZ

Dear Dr Sarah Nguyen,

Re: Alex Martin, DOB: 22-09-1983, Hospital No: B739204, NHS No. 1045829371
Flat 3, 27 Mill Street, Bristol, BS2 0PH

Diagnosis:

Seizure disorder under review with improving stability.

Aetiology:

No definitive structural or metabolic cause identified to date.

Other Medical Problems:

Vitamin D insufficiency previously noted; maintained on supplementation.

Clinical Summary:

Since our last review, the patient describes meaningful improvement in day-to-day stability. Night-time childcare support has been introduced, sleep has become more consolidated, and both the patient and partner report steadier mornings with fewer interruptions. In this context, No further tonic-clonic seizures have occurred since 06/2017, although three single jerks remain. These jerks were brief, without loss of awareness, and occurred during periods of sleep restriction earlier in the year. No injuries, tongue biting, incontinence, or prolonged post-event confusion have been reported. The patient continues working and notes improved concentration since sleep regularised.

Medication and Tolerability:

- Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily, well tolerated without rash, dizziness, or mood change.
- Levetiracetam previously trialled but stopped due to low mood; no plan to reintroduce.
- Vitamin D3 1000 IU daily, ongoing.

Investigations:

- Prior routine EEG and MRI brain were unremarkable; no new investigations requested today. If breakthrough events recur, we will reconsider ambulatory EEG.

Risk and Lifestyle:

- Driving: patient reports no qualifying events since 06/2017; advised to maintain seizure diary and to inform DVLA should any relevant events recur.
- Sleep: reinforced the benefit of consolidated sleep; night-time support appears to be effective and should be maintained.
- Safety: standard precautions around bathing at home; no bathing without supervision when overtired.

Management Plan:

1) Continue Lamotrigine 150 mg twice daily without brand switching, given current stability.
2) Maintain night-time childcare support to preserve sleep consolidation.
3) Keep a seizure and sleep diary, including any myoclonic jerks and precipitating factors.
4) Routine bloods (FBC, U&E, LFTs, Vitamin D) today for monitoring; we will inform the patient of any abnormalities.
5) Follow-up in 6 months, earlier if jerks increase in frequency, evolve, or any new events occur.

Overall, the trajectory is positive with improved sleep and day structure aligning with clinical stability.

Yours sincerely,

entities.text: No further tonic-clonic seizures have occurred since 06/2017, although three single jerks remain.
No further tonic-clonic seizures have occurred since 06/2017, although three single jerks remain

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 09 August 2018. The letter states there have been no tonic-clonic seizures since 06/2017, but there were three single jerks (treated as seizure events). These jerks occurred within the interval since 06/2017 up to the clinic date. Time window: from 06/2017 to 09/08/2018 ≈ 14 months. Total seizures in that span: 3. Therefore, frequency = 3 per 14 month.
